moveAnnotations.annotation: White claims the center. The Cow doesn't fight for the center directly — it lets White have it for now, builds behind the pawns, and waits for the strike.

First Cow move. Black declines to contest e4 directly. The pawn on e6 keeps the d5 break in reserve while leaving the light-squared bishop's diagonal closed for now — that bishop will route to b7 later.
plans: Pawns stay on e6 and d6 — the Cow's signature low-profile
Knights will cow-horn to b6 and g6 over the next 4-5 moves
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Black plays e6 — Cow setup begins.

moveAnnotations.annotation: Second Cow pawn. The e6/d6 wall makes the position look like a French — but the Cow's knights won't go to f6 or c6 like a French. They route via d7 and e7 to b6 and g6, the cow horns.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: d6 — the second Cow pawn. Knights route next.

moveAnnotations.annotation: First horn move. Nd7 here, not the usual Nc6 or Nf6, because the knight is en route to b6 via d7 — that's where the cow horn lives. From b6 the knight covers c4, d5, a4, and supports a future ...c5 break.
plans: Next: Ne7 (the other horn route) and Nb6 (completing the first horn)
Don't push pawns yet — let White overextend
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Knight to d7 — first horn route. b6 next.

moveAnnotations.annotation: Second horn route. The knight will swing to g6 — covering f4, h4, e5, and the king position. Looks slow, but the resulting structure is the most flexible Black can build.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Knight to e7 — second horn route, g6 next.

moveAnnotations.annotation: First horn LANDED. The knight on b6 looks passive but pressures c4 and d5, supports a future ...c5 strike, and chains with the eventual ...Bb7. White's bishop on d3 has to watch out for ...Na4 ideas if c2-c3 isn't played.
plans: After both horns are in place, look for the ...c5 or ...e5 break
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: First horn lands on b6 — covers c4 and d5.

moveAnnotations.annotation: Second horn LANDED. Both knights are on the cow's horns. Black's piece structure is fully built — every minor piece has a clear job, the pawns are still uncommitted, and the king will castle next. The Cow setup is COMPLETE. Now Black waits — White has to commit FIRST.
plans: Castle next (...Bg7 then ...O-O if the bishop fianchettos, otherwise direct ...Be7 + ...O-O)
Watch for the ...e5 break if White over-commits to the kingside
Watch for the ...c5 break against White's central pawns
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Both horns are home. The Cow waits — White has to swing first.
